heading: Authorization of circuit studies; education and training grants
label: 12381 Authorization of circuit studies; education and training grants
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to , The Public Health and Welfare, prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 14001 of Title 42

Amendments2000—Subsec. (d). amended heading and text of subsec. (d) generally, substituting provisions relating to continuing education and training programs for provisions relating to model programs.Pub. L. 106–386
amendments: Amendments2000—Subsec. (d). amended heading and text of subsec. (d) generally, substituting provisions relating to continuing education and training programs for provisions relating to model programs.Pub. L. 106–386

children.heading: Studies
children.label: a Studies
children.text: In order to gain a better understanding of the nature and the extent of gender bias in the Federal courts, the circuit judicial councils are encouraged to conduct studies of the instances, if any, of gender bias in their respective circuits and to implement recommended reforms.
the treatment of litigants, witnesses, attorneys, jurors, and judges in the courts, including before magistrate and bankruptcy judges;
the interpretation and application of the law, both civil and criminal;
treatment of defendants in criminal cases;
treatment of victims of violent crimes in judicial proceedings;
sentencing alternatives and the nature of supervision of probation and parole;
appointments to committees of the Judicial Conference and the courts;
case management and court sponsored alternative dispute resolution programs;
the selection, retention, promotion, and treatment of employees;
appointment of arbitrators, experts, and special masters;
the admissibility of the victim’s past sexual history in civil and criminal cases; and
the aspects of the topics listed in that pertain to issues within the jurisdiction of the Federal courts.section 12372 of this title

children.heading: Clearinghouse
children.label: c Clearinghouse
children.text: The Administrative Office of the United States Courts shall act as a clearinghouse to disseminate any reports and materials issued by the gender bias task forces under subsection (a) and to respond to requests for such reports and materials. The gender bias task forces shall provide the Administrative Office of the Courts of the United States with their reports and related material.1So in original. Probably should be “Administrative Office of the United States Courts”.1

children.heading: Continuing education and training programs
children.label: d Continuing education and training programs
children.text: The Federal Judicial Center, in carrying out , shall include in the educational programs it prepares, including the training programs for newly appointed judges, information on the aspects of the topics listed in that pertain to issues within the jurisdiction of the Federal courts, and shall prepare materials necessary to implement this subsection.section 620(b)(3) of title 28section 12372 of this title
